topic: A close-up of a Newton's Cradle in motion on a desk, macro view.
raw_generation:
{
  "script": "SCENE 1: DESK MACRO – NEWTON'S CRADLE IN MOTION
Shot 1: CLOSE UP ON THE TOP BEADS. Macro lens, shallow depth of field. Steel spheres, soft reflections on the desk surface.
Shot 2: SLOW MOTION. A single bead is lifted and released. You hear a crisp metallic click as it contacts the next bead.
Shot 3: The energy travels. The opposite end bead rises in a symmetric arc while the others remain briefly near rest.
Shot 4: CLOSER LOOK. A macro pass along the row shows the tiny gaps, the strings, and the precise alignment. Light ripples in the metal.
Shot 5: The cycle repeats. A subtle fade to the desk and a graphic overlay showing vectors of momentum and energy.
Sound design: metallic chimes, soft desk creak, very subtle air movement.",
  "narration": "On this desk, a Newton's cradle comes to life in macro detail. A single bead is lifted and released, and a crisp collision transfers momentum through the line of spheres. The far bead rises, and the motion reflects a fundamental principle: momentum and kinetic energy are conserved in nearly elastic collisions. With each pass, the amplitude slowly diminishes due to air resistance and internal friction, until the system rests again. The macro view reveals the geometry—the alignment of strings, the symmetry of the row, and the elegant transfer of energy from one end to the other."
}

parsed_generation.script:
SCENE 1: DESK MACRO – NEWTON'S CRADLE IN MOTION
Shot 1: CLOSE UP ON THE TOP BEADS. Macro lens, shallow depth of field. Steel spheres, soft reflections on the desk surface.
Shot 2: SLOW MOTION. A single bead is lifted and released. You hear a crisp metallic click as it contacts the next bead.
Shot 3: The energy travels. The opposite end bead rises in a symmetric arc while the others remain briefly near rest.
Shot 4: CLOSER LOOK. A macro pass along the row shows the tiny gaps, the strings, and the precise alignment. Light ripples in the metal.
Shot 5: The cycle repeats. A subtle fade to the desk and a graphic overlay showing vectors of momentum and energy.
Sound design: metallic chimes, soft desk creak, very subtle air movement.
parsed_generation.narration: On this desk, a Newton's cradle comes to life in macro detail. A single bead is lifted and released, and a crisp collision transfers momentum through the line of spheres. The far bead rises, and the motion reflects a fundamental principle: momentum and kinetic energy are conserved in nearly elastic collisions. With each pass, the amplitude slowly diminishes due to air resistance and internal friction, until the system rests again. The macro view reveals the geometry—the alignment of strings, the symmetry of the row, and the elegant transfer of energy from one end to the other.
